hero_description: Learn professional waiter and restaurant service skills to work in hotels, restaurants, and catering establishments across India. This 1-month foundation course covers table service, customer interaction, food safety, and hospitality standards that are in high demand in India's growing food service industry.
overview_description: This course trains candidates to deliver excellent customer service in restaurant and hospitality settings. You will learn proper table setup, order taking, food and beverage service techniques, hygiene standards, and communication skills required to work in 3-star to 5-star restaurants, hotels, and catering companies. Practical hands-on training ensures you are job-ready from day one.

modules.title: Restaurant Service Fundamentals & Industry Overview
topics: Introduction to hospitality industry in India — types of restaurants, hotels, and catering establishments
Waiter role and responsibilities — job scope, career progression, and expectations
Restaurant hierarchy and communication — understanding chain of command from manager to kitchen staff
Types of service styles — French service, American service, Russian service, and English service basics
Restaurant layout and sections — understanding dining areas, kitchen flow, and service stations
Health and safety regulations — Indian food safety standards and FSSAI compliance basics
Professional ethics and code of conduct — punctuality, dress code, and customer confidentiality
Introduction to restaurant technology — POS systems, billing software, and basic digital ordering

modules.title: Table Setup, Dining Etiquette & Service Basics
topics: Table setup and laying — correct placement of plates, glasses, cutlery for different meal types
Napkin folding and placement — basic and advanced folding techniques for formal dining
Glassware and crockery knowledge — types of glasses, plates, bowls, and their proper usage
Cutlery handling — understanding forks, knives, spoons, and their correct order of use
Table numbering and section management — organizing tables for efficient service
Greeting and seating guests — welcoming customers, checking reservations, and table assignment
Dining etiquette standards — understanding formal dining rules and guest expectations
Service techniques — proper posture, hand positioning, and left-hand service method
Table maintenance during service — clearing, resetting, and keeping tables clean
Special occasion table setup — birthdays, anniversaries, corporate events, and themed setups

modules.title: Order Taking, Menu Knowledge & Communication Skills
topics: Menu knowledge essentials — understanding dishes, ingredients, and preparation methods
Reading and interpreting menus — vegetarian, non-vegetarian, vegan, and special diet options
Taking orders accurately — listening skills, clarifying preferences, and noting special requests
Order communication to kitchen — using order tickets, kitchen terminology, and timing
Upselling and suggestive selling — recommending appetizers, beverages, and desserts professionally
Handling dietary restrictions — allergies, vegetarian preferences, and religious dietary needs
Managing special requests — customizations, portion sizes, and modification requests
Handling customer complaints about orders — problem-solving and escalation procedures
Beverage service basics — water service, soft drinks, juices, and traditional Indian beverages
Taking payment and billing — understanding bills, GST, service charges, and payment methods

modules.title: Food & Beverage Service Techniques & Safety Standards
topics: Food service standards — temperature control, presentation, and plating basics
Serving food correctly — right-hand service, proper plating, and avoiding contamination
Beverage service techniques — serving water, soft drinks, tea, coffee, and hot beverages
Alcoholic beverage service — understanding beer, wine, and spirits service (if applicable venue)
Clearing plates and course management — timing between courses, clearing rules, and waste management
Food hygiene and safety — handwashing, cross-contamination prevention, and personal cleanliness
FSSAI compliance and food safety standards — understanding Indian food safety regulations
Handling food allergies and contamination risks — identifying allergens and preventing incidents
Temperature maintenance during service — keeping hot food hot and cold food cold
Waste management and sustainability — proper disposal and environmental responsibility
Handling food waste and leftover management — Indian restaurant standards and regulations
Emergency procedures — handling spills, broken items, and customer safety

modules.title: Customer Service Excellence & Conflict Management
topics: Customer service philosophy — understanding guest expectations and delivering excellence
Communication skills for hospitality — polite language, tone, and non-verbal communication
Handling difficult customers — patience, empathy, and de-escalation techniques
Managing complaints professionally — listening, apologizing, and offering solutions
Building customer relationships — remembering preferences and creating repeat business
Multilingual service basics — essential phrases in Hindi, English, and regional languages
Cultural sensitivity in Indian hospitality — respecting diverse customs and preferences
Managing rush hours and high-pressure situations — staying calm and organized
Feedback handling and continuous improvement — learning from customer suggestions
VIP and special guest handling — recognizing important customers and providing premium service

modules.title: Professional Development, Workplace Skills & Career Planning
topics: Personal grooming and appearance standards — uniform maintenance, hygiene, and presentation
Time management and punctuality — scheduling, shift management, and reliability
Teamwork and collaboration — working with chefs, managers, and other service staff
Stress management in hospitality — handling pressure, fatigue, and emotional demands
Learning from experienced staff — mentorship, feedback, and skill development
Career progression pathways — moving from waiter to captain, supervisor, or management
Certification and skill upgrades — advanced service techniques and specialized training
Job search strategies in India — finding positions in hotels, restaurants, and catering companies
Understanding employment contracts and labor rights — wages, working hours, and benefits
Building professional portfolio — references, certifications, and performance records

assessment.theory_format: 25 Questions x 2 Marks (Multiple Choice & Short Answer)
assessment.practical_format: Hands-on demonstration of restaurant service skills in simulated and real restaurant environments

practical_items.title: Table Setup & Service Demonstration
practical_items.description: Candidate must set up a complete table with proper placement of crockery, cutlery, glasses, and napkins. Then demonstrate greeting, seating, and initial water service to mock guests. Evaluated on accuracy, professionalism, and efficiency.

practical_items.title: Order Taking & Communication
practical_items.description: Candidate takes orders from mock customers, clarifies special requests, communicates orders to kitchen staff, and handles menu queries. Assessed on listening skills, accuracy, upselling ability, and customer interaction quality.

practical_items.title: Food & Beverage Service
practical_items.description: Candidate serves prepared food and beverages using correct techniques (right-hand service, proper positioning). Demonstrates clearing plates, maintaining table cleanliness, and managing multiple courses. Evaluated on technique, hygiene, and professionalism.

practical_items.title: Customer Service & Problem Solving
practical_items.description: Candidate handles mock customer scenarios including complaints, special requests, and difficult situations. Must demonstrate empathy, professional communication, and problem-solving ability. Assessed on response quality and customer satisfaction approach.

eligibility: Minimum age: 14 years (with parental consent for 14-16 years)
Minimum education: Class 8th pass or equivalent
No prior experience needed — beginners welcome
Already working as a waiter? Apply for RPL (Recognition of Prior Learning) exam
Valid ID proof required at registration (Aadhaar, PAN, Voter ID, or Passport)
Medical fitness certificate may be required by some employers
Applicable for all Indian citizens and foreign nationals with valid visa
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate professional table setup and service techniques according to international standards
Take accurate food and beverage orders while providing menu recommendations and handling special requests
Serve food and beverages using correct techniques while maintaining hygiene and safety standards
Communicate effectively with customers, colleagues, and kitchen staff in professional hospitality settings
Handle customer complaints and difficult situations with professionalism, empathy, and problem-solving skills
Understand food safety, FSSAI compliance, and health regulations relevant to Indian restaurants

course_highlights.practical_training: 80% of course is hands-on training in simulated restaurant environments with real service scenarios
course_highlights.industry_partnerships: Trained by professionals from 3-5 star hotels and established restaurant chains across India
course_highlights.job_placement_support: Placement assistance with partner restaurants, hotels, and catering companies in your city
course_highlights.certification_validity: CETCF certification recognized across India for employment in hospitality sector
course_highlights.flexible_timing: Weekend and evening batches available for working professionals
course_highlights.language_options: Training available in Hindi, English, and regional languages

course_benefits: Industry-recognized CETCF certification that increases job prospects across India
Practical skills that make you immediately employable in restaurants, hotels, and catering
Competitive salary starting from ₹12,000/month with growth potential to ₹70,000+
Job opportunities in metro cities, tier-2 cities, and even international cruise ships
Flexible career path — advance to supervisor, manager, or start your own catering business
Soft skills training in communication, customer service, and professional etiquette
Understanding of Indian food safety standards and FSSAI compliance requirements

job_market_demand.growth_rate: 15-20% annual growth in India's hospitality sector
job_market_demand.key_employers: ITC Hotels, Taj Hotels, Oberoi Group, Marriott, Hyatt, local 5-star hotels, fine dining restaurants, cloud kitchens, catering companies
job_market_demand.top_hiring_cities: Mumbai, Delhi, Bangalore, Hyderabad, Pune, Chennai, Kolkata, Jaipur, Ahmedabad
job_market_demand.employment_type: Full-time, Part-time, Seasonal (events and weddings), Contract-based
job_market_demand.international_opportunities: Cruise ships, international hotel chains, Middle East hospitality sector

additional_resources.study_materials: Printed workbooks, video tutorials, menu samples, service guides, FSSAI guidelines
additional_resources.practice_environments: Fully equipped training kitchen, mock dining areas, POS system training
additional_resources.mentorship: Guidance from experienced restaurant managers and hospitality professionals
additional_resources.alumni_network: Connect with graduates working in top hotels and restaurants across India
